week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 9-1 demolition in Week 1, balanced offense with four HR hitters, three solid SP performances — the most complete team in the league right now | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Trout (6 R, 3 HR, 5 RBI, .625 OBP) and Murakami both went deep three times in a week; offense is historically dangerous and 8-2 reflects it | same

3. General Hospital | Tied despite 23 RBI, .414 OBP, and 6 HR — this roster is elite and the tie feels more like bad luck than a weakness; adding Kumar Rocker deepens an already strong rotation | same
4. North Correa | Chris Sale was untouchable (15 K, 0.75 WHIP, 2 QS), Ohtani contributed on both sides, and a 7-3 win over the Reapers shows legitimate depth | up
5. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge and Will Smith went deep, Sanchez was filthy (0.50 WHIP), and a 5-4 gritty win over Hiroshima shows this team can grind out victories | same
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Seven HR, three QS, and a 2.98 ERA in a tie — the pitching is real, the offense is emerging, and Brendan Donovan's multi-position versatility is a huge roster asset | up
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Alvarez and Aranda mashed, 43 K from the staff, but a 6.00 ERA and zero QS in 39 IP cost them dearly; adding Merrill Kelly is the right move | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Lost 4-5 despite 44 K and a 2.35 ERA — Skubal and Bradley were sharp, but Eovaldi's 9.64 ERA and a completely frozen stolen base column killed them | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 0.33 WHIP and Yelich's .600 OBP were electric; four aggressive waiver adds signal a manager who knows this roster needs reinforcing | same
10. Benchwarmers | Lucky 7-3 win masks a thin roster, but Michael King (14 K, 1.64 ERA) and Spencer Arrighetti (8 K, 1.29 ERA) give them a real two-ace foundation | up
11. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease was sharp (1.69 ERA) but Aaron Nola got rocked (8.38 ERA); an 0-HR week in a power-scoring league is a brutal structural flaw | down
12. Riverside Roughnecks | Lost 1-9 in the most lopsided result of the week; Langeliers hit 3 HR but the pitching was historically bad (7.91 ERA, 0 QS, 19.1 IP) | down
13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 39 K is impressive but Matthew Boyd's 14.73 ERA and Tyler Wells' 10.80 ERA are disqualifying; only 1 HR all week in a power league is alarming | same
14. Buttville Skidmarks | DeLauter slugging 4 HR can't paper over a .271 OBP and 6.02 ERA — until the pitching stabilizes, this team will keep losing winnable matchups | same
week_02: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | League-best 40 R, 8-1 blowout, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both erupted — three straight top-tier weeks cements top spot | same
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 9-0 sweep is the statement of the season; Ben Rice, Mason Miller, and a 0.84 WHIP rotation demand respect | up
3. General Hospital | 2.10 ERA, .363 OBP, and a 6-4 win despite -0.52 luck delta — this team is quietly undervalued and primed to surge | same
4. BaseballPro77 | Ugly offensive week (2 HR, .277 OBP) but pitching bailed them out; a 5-4 win is a 5-4 win and they remain elite on the mound | down
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez went nuclear (.600 OBP, 3 HR, 8 RBI), league-record-tying 77 K, and a 2.25 ERA — Merrill Kelly add could be pivotal | up
6. North Correa | Oneil Cruz (4 HR, 10 RBI) and Ohtani (.441 OBP) powered a 7-3 win; pitching ERA of 2.85 rounds out a balanced roster | same
7. Riverside Roughnecks | 38 runs, 10 HR, and a 7-3 victory — this team has quietly become one of the most explosive offenses in the league | up
8. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Torres all contributing — thin pitching (46 IP) remains the Achilles heel | down
9. Hiroshima Radiation | 8-1 dismantling is a brutal wake-up call; Carlos Correa and Schwarber are fine but the team gave up 40 runs and only 1 category | down
10. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost 7-3 but Kyle Harrison (26 K, 1.65 ERA) was a revelation; aggressive waiver activity shows a manager fighting back | same
11. Canberra Wombats | Altuve (.577 OBP), Henderson (3 HR), and Walker (3 HR) all hit — but pitching (Seth Lugo 7.15 ERA) remains a disaster | up
12. North Correa already ranked — Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite solid pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS); offense is too passive and Reynolds' production is being wasted | down
13. Benchwarmers | Carroll was a one-man army but the roster around him is still thin; barely escaped with a 5-4 win against a struggling opponent | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9-0 shutout loss with zero category wins is as bad as it gets; the offense (3 HR, 13 RBI) is an emergency | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Three weeks of elite production, historically low ERA, balanced offense led by Olson and Baldwin — this is the league's best team | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5-HR explosion anchors a team winning close matchups; pitching staff has quietly been reliable across all three weeks | same
3. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood and Willson Contreras put up absurd numbers (Wood: .636 OBP; Contreras: 11 RBI) in a convincing win; Judge adds elite depth | up
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Flipped the script with an 8–2 blowout, leading the league with 6 QS and strong across-the-board pitching; Bryce Harper (.552 OBP) heating up | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost despite a 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP — elite pitching but bats must produce more; strong underlying metrics keep them in the top half | down
6. General Hospital | Most unlucky team this week; a tie when their expected wins say they should have won outright — talent is real, results haven't followed | down
7. North Correa | Chris Sale's 22-K, 16-IP week is a monster performance and Ohtani remains elite, but offense ranked near the bottom with only 18 runs scored | same
8. Riverside Roughnecks | Manager spotlight week delivered a 6–3 win; Trevor Story's 9 RBI and zero quality starts somehow worked — sustainability is the question | up
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Acuña, Turang, and Sal Stewart all had strong individual weeks but the pitching (5.05 ERA) continues to sink the ship | same
10. Buttville Skidmarks | A tie feels like a loss when your ERA is 2.25; offense needs to wake up — only 22 RBI and 7 HR in a week where runs were scarce | down
11. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Active waiver week (Masyn Winn, Dustin May added) signals urgency; Imanaga was excellent but the offense is a real liability at .306 OBP | up
12. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8–2 despite decent team counting stats; pitching gave up runs in bunches and the Merrill Kelly add needs to pay off fast | down
13. Benchwarmers | Won this week but with the worst pitching profile among winners; 42.1 IP and a 5.53 ERA is not a formula for sustained success | same
14. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson's 5-HR week was spectacular but the team still lost 8–2; getting crushed in pitching categories weekly is a fatal flaw | same
week_04: 1. BaseballPro77 | League-record 19 HR and 39 RBI in a single week; Trout is playing like an MVP and the pitching is holding up — this is the team to beat | up
2. General Hospital | League-best 8 category wins this week, 77.1 IP, 6 QS, and a 2.68 ERA; Will Warren and a deep rotation make this team quietly terrifying | same
3. Sugar Land Skeeters | Took a loss but remain the most consistent top-tier performer across all four weeks; Tatis, Happ, and Weathers give them multi-category upside every night | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge's 6-HR week headlines a 5-2 win; Cristopher Sánchez and Bailey Ober both looked sharp and this pitching staff is deepening | same
5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | League-leading 8 QS and a 2.49 ERA remain the calling card; Paul Skenes' 0.90 ERA and Imanaga bouncing back make the rotation elite | same
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Ramirez's .500 OBP and 4-HR week was jaw-dropping; bad luck continues to suppress the record but the talent is undeniable | up
7. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean and Eovaldi both posted 1.50 ERAs and 5 combined QS — the rotation is for real, but the offense remains the Achilles heel | down
8. North Correa | Chris Sale's 0.69 ERA and 16 K effort was elite; Ohtani and Hoerner give them a high floor even if the pitching outside Sale is inconsistent | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Buxton led a solid offensive week and the team won 5 categories; the wave of waiver additions shows active management trying to patch real holes | same
10. Riverside Roughnecks | Tied when they likely should have won; Langeliers, Kurtz, and Misiorowski are legitimate pieces but this team needs more consistency across the lineup | same
11. Canberra Wombats | A 4-4 tie is better than a loss but Gunnar Henderson's .189 OBP and a thin roster ceiling remain serious concerns heading into a tough stretch | up
12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got torched 7-2 by the hottest team in the league; Ben Rice remains a genuine bright spot but 33 hits and a .257 OBP are roster-wide problems | down
13. Buttville Skidmarks | Reynolds' .500 OBP was completely wasted again as the pitching posted a 4.87 ERA; this team loses in the same way every week | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 19 K and 1.59 ERA are genuinely great but the rest of this roster — 4 HR, 45 K, bottom-five in nearly every offensive category — is not viable without significant additions | same
